title: What's new in the World Development Indicators: July 2026 update
gist: The World Bank's July 2026 World Development Indicators refresh delivers 2025 national accounts, poverty estimates through 2024, the FY27 country income classification, updated CPIA scores, and new gender, displacement, and fisheries data across the Open Data site, DataBank, and API.

insights.claim: The July 2026 WDI update introduces the FY27 country income classification, which sets the thresholds separating low-, middle-, and high-income economies.
insights.speaker: World Bank Data Team
insights.so_what: Income-classification changes can trigger shifts in concessional-lending eligibility and index inclusion, worth checking for reclassified sovereigns.
insights.quote: The database now features the FY27 country income classification.

insights.claim: The update adds national accounts statistics for 2025 (GDP and components, expenditure aggregates, sectoral value added, income measures) plus refreshed PPP conversion factors using the latest deflator and CPI data.
insights.speaker: World Bank Data Team
insights.so_what: Fresh 2025 GDP and PPP data update the baseline for cross-country real-income and relative-valuation comparisons.

insights.claim: Country Policy and Institutional Assessment (CPIA) scores for 2025 are now available, rating countries on economic management, structural policies, public sector management, and social inclusion.
insights.speaker: World Bank Data Team
insights.so_what: CPIA scores feed IDA allocation and sovereign risk assessment, so updated ratings can move concessional-finance flows.
insights.locator: The Country Policy and Institutional Assessment

insights.claim: Poverty estimates have been extended through 2024, including headcount ratios and poverty gaps for upper-middle, lower-middle, and low-income country groups, while population indicators are updated through 2025.
insights.speaker: World Bank Data Team
insights.so_what: Updated poverty and demographic series refine market-sizing and consumer-base estimates for emerging economies.

insights.claim: Forced displacement indicators tracking refugees, asylum-seekers, and internally displaced persons by country of asylum and origin have been updated.
insights.speaker: World Bank Data Team
insights.so_what: Refreshed displacement data helps quantify humanitarian and labor-flow pressures across host and origin countries.

insights.claim: Financial and macroeconomic indicators were updated with the latest IMF data, strengthening coverage of credit conditions and financial inclusion, alongside refreshed foreign investment flows, remittances, and enterprise survey data on licensing times and power outages affecting firms.
insights.speaker: World Bank Data Team
insights.so_what: Enterprise-survey metrics like power-outage frequency and licensing times are practical proxies for business-climate and FDI attractiveness.

insights.claim: Fisheries production data has been refreshed using the latest FAO figures as the environmental component of the update.
insights.speaker: World Bank Data Team
insights.so_what: Updated fisheries output data supports food-security and blue-economy analysis for coastal and island economies.
